nodes.name: 艾爾湖
nodes.summary: 艾爾湖是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括斑长脚鹬、Eyrean grasswren，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鹹水湖
nodes.summary: 鹹水湖是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括斑长脚鹬、Snowy plover、Wilson's phalarope等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 菲利普港湾

nodes.name: 鸻形目
nodes.summary: 鸻形目（Charadriiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联310种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、短腿鸻、白鞘嘴鸥等310种鸟类。

nodes.name: 斑长脚鹬
nodes.summary: 斑长脚鹬（Banded stilt，学名 Cladorhynchus leucocephalus），分类上属于鸻形目，并归入反嘴鹬科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联12个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、滩涂。
locations: 瑞福利纳 (新南威尔士州)

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 反嘴鹬科
nodes.summary: 反嘴鹬科（Recurvirostridae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如斑长脚鹬、美洲反嘴鹬、Andean avocet等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.key: bird-斑长脚鹬-c39a1e57__distributed_in__loc-路易-皮埃尔-维埃约-d872b74a
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Gray]], 1840 | species = leucocephalus | authority = (Vieillot, 1816) |synonyms = Recurvirostra leucocephala Vieillot Recurvirostra orientalis Cuvier, 1816 Leptorhynchus pectoralis Du Bus, 1835 Himantopus palmatus Gould Cladorhynchus australis Lawson Cladorhynchus leucocephalus rottnesti Mathews Xiphidiorhynchus pectoralis Ludwig Reichenbach, 1845 |synonyms_ref = | range_map = Bandedstiltrgemap.png | range_map_caption = Banded stilt natural range }} The banded stilt (Cladorhynchus leucocephalus) is a nomadic wader of the stilt and avocet family, Recurvirostridae, native to Australia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | journal=Emu |volume= 33 | issue= 2|pages= 103–09|year= 1933 |doi=10.1071/MU933103 | bibcode=1933EmuAO..33..103C }} The banded stilt has been recorded in southeastern South Australia, as well as the drainage of the Lake Eyre system, and in Victoria west of Port Phillip and the Wimmera. In July 2010 Lake Torrens filled with water, resulting in the influx of around 150,000 banded stilts. The Natimuk-Douglas Wetlands in western Victoria are an important nesting ground for the species, though lower numbers come here if there is flooding elsewhere in southeastern Australia. In New South Wales, it is most commonly found in the Riverina and western parts of the state, and has reached southern Queensland and the Northern Territory, where it has been found at the sewage ponds at Alice Springs and Erldunda. It has been recorded as a vagrant to Tasmania, with significant numbers recorded in 1981. The preferred habitats are large, shallow saline or hypersaline lakes, either inland or near the coast, including ephemeral salt lakes, salt works, lagoons, salt- or claypans and intertidal flats
